event: history of primary refractory acute myeloblastic leukemia
underwent allogeneic bone marrow transplant from cord blood
treated with tedizolid, cefepime, Flagyl and daptomycin
prophylaxis with acyclovir, Bactrim, and caspofungin
anemic with a hemoglobin of 7 g/L
thrombocytopenic with a platelet count of 10 x 109 /L
CT scan revealed segmental ischemia of the small bowel
required norepinephrine and vasopressin for blood pressure support
admitted to the intensive care unit overnight
pressors were weaned and he was extubated the following day
diet was advanced on postoperative day (POD) 3
new fevers, with increased abdominal pain, lactic acidosis and respiratory decompensation
continued to be neutropenic with white cell count of 0.1x 109 /L and ANC 0
lactic acid at that time was 3.7 mmol/L
repeat CT scan showed an area of necrotic small bowel
invasive fungal forms in both the omental and small intestinal resection specimens
Amphotericin B was added to his antimicrobials
risks of a repeat exploratory laparotomy and bowel resection were deemed quite high
the decision was made to proceed to comfort measures
